TITLE: The Big Sleep
AUTHOR: Raymond Chandler
SYNOPSIS: Philip Marlowe takes a case for a dying general and walks into a Los Angeles underworld of blackmail and murder where every solution uncovers two new problems. The plot famously doesn't fully resolve, and it doesn't need to.

TITLE: The Maltese Falcon
AUTHOR: Dashiell Hammett
SYNOPSIS: When Sam Spade's partner is killed while tailing a client, the trail leads to a priceless jeweled bird and every kind of liar. The novel that established the private eye as a moral figure in an immoral world.

TITLE: Farewell, My Lovely
AUTHOR: Raymond Chandler
SYNOPSIS: Marlowe is pulled into a search for an ex-con's missing girlfriend and ends up entangled in jewel theft, a corrupt cop, and a society murder. Many critics consider it Chandler's finest construction, and they are not wrong.

TITLE: The Long Goodbye
AUTHOR: Raymond Chandler
SYNOPSIS: A chance friendship with a charming drunk leads Marlowe into a case that strips away every comfortable illusion about loyalty, wealth, and love. Longer, slower, and more melancholy than the earlier books -- the one where Chandler lets himself be sad.

TITLE: Red Harvest
AUTHOR: Dashiell Hammett
SYNOPSIS: The Continental Op arrives in a company-owned mining town so thoroughly corrupt that the only way to clean it out is to play every faction against the others until they destroy each other. Lean, violent, and morally uncomfortable.

TITLE: I, the Jury
AUTHOR: Mickey Spillane
SYNOPSIS: Mike Hammer's best friend is murdered and Hammer vows to deliver the punishment himself, not through a courtroom but with his own hand. Spillane wrote this in nine days. It feels like it, in the best possible sense.

TITLE: The Moving Target
AUTHOR: Ross Macdonald
SYNOPSIS: Lew Archer is hired to find a missing millionaire and discovers a California family rotting from the inside out. The first Archer novel and an immediate argument that Macdonald deserved to stand alongside Hammett and Chandler.

TITLE: The Chill
AUTHOR: Ross Macdonald
SYNOPSIS: A young husband hires Archer to find his runaway bride, and the trail leads back through two decades of buried crimes toward a secret someone will kill again to protect. The novel in which Macdonald's method -- archaeology as detective work -- reaches its fullest expression.

TITLE: Devil in a Blue Dress
AUTHOR: Walter Mosley
SYNOPSIS: Easy Rawlins, an unemployed black veteran in 1948 Los Angeles, is hired to find a white woman who likes jazz clubs on Central Avenue, and stumbles into something far more dangerous -- and more revealing about postwar America -- than anyone warned him.
